tests.prompt: Run /daily for 2026-04-03 (evening). Today's Telegram messages include: 'Had a call with Gerard about grad program timeline - he wants to push to Q3', 'TalentSignals demo went well, Andrew closing the lead', 'Content idea: how corporate TA leaders actually use AI day to day', and a voice note '[Voice transcribed]: Met with the SJM procurement team about ATS renewal, they want three vendor options by Friday'. Yesterday's daily note (2026-04-02) has open loops: '- [ ] Follow up with Anna on JD draft' and '- [ ] Review ATS vendor shortlist'.
tests.expected_output: A compiled daily note with SJM Work, Signhub/TalentSignals, Content Seeds, and Open Loops sections. Should ask clarifying questions before saving.
assertions: Does the output use the file path Daily/2026-04-03.md?
Does the YAML frontmatter include tags with [daily] and the date 2026-04-03?
Does it classify the Gerard call and ATS renewal under SJM Work (not under another section)?
Does it classify the TalentSignals demo under Signhub / TalentSignals / Agency at Scale?
Does it classify the content idea under Content Seeds?
Does it carry forward at least one open loop from yesterday?
Does K2B ask at least one clarifying question about the captures before saving?
Are empty sections omitted (no K2B Build section since there was no build activity)?
Does it treat the voice transcription the same as regular text messages?

tests.prompt: Run /daily for 2026-04-04 (evening). No Telegram messages today. One TLDR exists in raw/tldrs/: '2026-04-04_tldr-daily-redesign.md' about redesigning the /daily skill. Yesterday's daily note has open loop: '- [ ] Send ATS vendor shortlist to procurement'. No other vault notes created today.
tests.expected_output: A minimal daily note with just K2B Build and Open Loops. Should note the quiet day and ask if anything was missed.
assertions: Does it include K2B Build section referencing the daily redesign work?
Does it carry forward the ATS vendor shortlist open loop?
Does it omit SJM Work section (no SJM captures)?
Does it omit Signhub / TalentSignals section (no side venture captures)?
Does K2B ask something like 'quiet day for SJM - anything I missed?' or similar?
Does each section use bullet points, not paragraphs?

tests.prompt: Run /daily for 2026-04-05 (morning). No Telegram messages yet today. Yesterday's daily note (2026-04-04) has open loops: '- [ ] Send ATS vendor shortlist to procurement' and '- [ ] Follow up with Andrew on TalentSignals pricing'.
tests.expected_output: A brief morning mode response showing yesterday's open loops and inviting Keith to capture throughout the day.
assertions: Does it show yesterday's open loops (ATS vendor shortlist and TalentSignals pricing)?
Does it link to yesterday's note as [[2026-04-04]]?
Does it NOT create a full compiled daily note (morning mode should be brief)?
Does it suggest running /daily again later to compile?
Does the filename follow YYYY-MM-DD.md convention?

tests.prompt: Run /daily for 2026-04-06 (evening). Telegram messages: 'Spent the whole day at the recruitment fair, talked to maybe 200 candidates', 'The booth design was terrible, need to escalate to marketing', '[Voice transcribed]: Quick thought - the grad program pipeline numbers are actually better than last year, might be worth a LinkedIn post about how we turned it around'. No yesterday open loops. No TLDRs.
tests.expected_output: A daily note with SJM Work, Content Seeds, and possibly Insights. All from Telegram captures only.
assertions: Does it classify recruitment fair activity under SJM Work?
Does it classify the LinkedIn post idea under Content Seeds?
Does it handle the voice transcription without treating it differently from text?
Are there no empty sections with blank bullets?
Does it ask at least one question (e.g., about the marketing escalation or any open loops)?
